---
title: An introduction to JAX
description: High-performance array computing and differentiation with [![](jx/img/logo_jax.png){width="1.7em"}](https://jax.readthedocs.io/)
title-block-banner: true
---

:::{.topdef}

[JAX](https://github.com/jax-ml/jax) is an open source Python library for high-performance array computing and flexible automatic differentiation.

High-performance computing is achieved by [asynchronous dispatch](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asynchrony_(computer_programming)), [just-in-time compilation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Just-in-time_compilation), the [XLA compiler for linear algebra](https://www.tensorflow.org/xla), and full compatibility with accelerators ([GPUs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graphics_processing_unit) and [TPUs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tensor_Processing_Unit)).

Automatic differentiation uses [Autograd](https://github.com/hips/autograd) and works with complex control flows (conditions, recursions), second and third-order derivatives, forward and reverse modes. This makes JAX ideal for machine learning and neural network libraries such as [Flax](https://flax.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) are built on it.

:::

<br>
[[Start course ➤](jx/jx_why.qmd)]{.topinline}

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LearningResource",
  "name": "High-performance array computing and differentiation with JAX",
  "description": "JAX is an open source Python library for high-performance array computing and flexible automatic differentiation.\n\nHigh-performance computing is achieved by asynchronous dispatch, just-in-time compilation, the XLA compiler for linear algebra, and full compatibility with accelerators (GPUs and TPUs).\n\nAutomatic differentiation uses Autograd and works with complex control flows (conditions, recursions), second and third-order derivatives, forward and reverse modes. This makes JAX ideal for machine learning and neural network libraries such as Flax are built on it.",
  "url": "https://mint.westdri.ca/ai/top_jx",
  "learningResourceType": "course",
  "inLanguage": "en",
  "license": "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/",
  "provider": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SFU Research Computing Group"
  },
  "keywords": "JAX, Python, automatic differentiation, GPU, TPU, accelerator",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Marie-Hélène Burle"
  },
  "educationalLevel": "beginner"
}
</script>
